label: PASS
rationale: MBRControl was set with Enable true, Done true, and MBRDoneOnReset containing Power Cycle. The subsequent PowerCycle reset must set Done false, so with MBREnabled still 1 the reported MBRDone 0 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: MBRControl was set with Enable true, Done true, and MBRDoneOnReset containing Power Cycle. A PowerCycle reset is listed and must set Done false, so Level0 MBRDone should be 0, not 1.

label: PASS
rationale: MBRControl was set with Enable true, Done true, and an empty DoneOnReset set. The evidence says an empty set must not change Done on any reset, so after PowerCycle Done remains true and MBRDone 1 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: MBRControl was set with Enable true, Done true, and an empty DoneOnReset set. Because an empty DoneOnReset must not change Done on any reset, PowerCycle should leave Done true and Level0 MBRDone should be 1, not 0.

label: PASS
rationale: MBRControl was set with Enable true, Done true, and DoneOnReset containing Power Cycle and Programmatic. The subsequent TPerReset corresponds to the programmatic reset type in this evidence set, so Done must become false and MBRDone 0 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: MBRControl was set with Enable true, Done true, and DoneOnReset containing Power Cycle and Programmatic. The TPerReset is the programmatic reset event for this scenario, so Done must be reset false and Level0 MBRDone should be 0, not 1.

label: PASS
rationale: MBRControl was set with Enable true and Done false. MBRDone is one only when MBREnabled is one and Done is true; with Done false and empty DoneOnReset preserving that value, the reported MBRDone 0 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: MBRControl was set with Enable true but Done false. Since MBRDone can be one only when MBREnabled is one and Done is true, and an empty DoneOnReset does not change Done on PowerCycle, reporting MBRDone 1 is non-compliant.
